_meta.author: Claude (Adam-supervised, autopilot local cron, 1550 batch)
_meta.note:
VA TIDEWATER LOCAL batch — 5 officials across Norfolk (3) + Portsmouth (2). Norfolk: courtney-doyle (Council Ward 2, I→D-aligned, Catholic at Sacred Heart Norfolk, on council since 2018), jeremy-mcgee (Council Superward 6, I→D-aligned, Navy veteran + 'home preacher,' sworn 1/1/2025), martin-thomas-jr (Vice Mayor since 2018 + Council Ward 1 since Aug 2016, I→D-aligned, Catholic by St. Mary's Catholic Cemetery board service inference). Portsmouth: kathryn-bryant (At-Large Council, I, Episcopalian — Director of Children & Family Ministry at Trinity Episcopal Portsmouth, a TEC LGBT-affirming parish, sworn 1/1/2025), mark-hugel (At-Large Council, I, retired RADM USN ret. — 100th Commander Norfolk Naval Shipyard, sworn 1/1/2023, running for re-election Nov 2026). KEY EVIDENCE: (1) Doyle — HARD FALSE bib_marriage q4: Dec 12 2023 Armed Forces Brewing Co. permit debate she stated promotional videos and social media tied to the brewery 'do not respect the LGBTQ community, women or our Norfolk values' — affirmative endorsement of LGBTQ as Norfolk civic value. HARD TRUE econ_q2: voted YES on May 14 2024 FY25 budget that CUT real-estate rate 2¢ from $1.25 to $1.23. HARD FALSE pj_q3: stated approach to gun violence is using 'public education system, recreation centers, libraries, and community groups to impress upon youth that guns are not the solution' — textbook root-causes/public-health framing. HARD FALSE 2A_q1: supports 'constructive and meaningful gun legislation that prevents guns from getting into the hands of those who shouldn't possess a gun' — affirmative gun-control framing. SOFT FALSE refuse_overreach_q2: signature platform issue is climate/sea-level-rise leadership ('positioned our city as a leader in combating' climate challenges) per Progressive Voters Guide credit; ESG-affirmative direction even absent a specific climate-emergency-resolution vote cite. NOTE: Dec 2023 Armed Forces Brewing yes-vote on free-speech grounds (her quote: 'oath to uphold the right to free speech despite the fact I disagree with the speech') is a content-neutral free-speech defense, NOT specifically Christian-business religious-liberty per strict rubric — left christian_liberty q0 NULL with the cite preserved in notes for audit trail. (2) McGee — HARD TRUE econ_q2: campaign housing page (https://www.mcgeefornorfolk.com/housing) states 'I supported the recent property tax rate reduction and believe we should continue to carefully consider the impact of property taxes on housing affordability'; he assumed office 1/1/2025 (FY25 May 2024 rate cut PREDATES his tenure so he didn't cast that vote himself), but as a sitting member voted YES on the May 12 2026 FY26 budget that held the rate at $1.23 (no increase) per Norfolk meeting minutes. Sparse-data caveat acknowledged in notes (1 cited TRUE / 0 FALSE on 30 cells = 100% on cited but reflects research diligence not actual breadth). (3) Thomas Jr — HARD TRUE pj_q0 (HIGH BAR cleared): law-firm professional bio (https://petedecker.com/martin-thomas-jr/) states he 'has successfully championed increases in pay for public safety employees' AND Norfolk's FY25 budget he voted for delivered a 10%+ raise for nearly 300 Norfolk police officers — affirmative pro-LE advocacy on a multi-vector basis (his own quote + the corresponding budget vote). HARD TRUE econ_q2: voted YES on May 2024 FY25 budget 2¢ rate cut. NOTE on christian_liberty q4: agent initially flagged Armed Forces Brewing 6-1 vote as TRUE then correctly downgraded to NULL because (a) Thomas issued no individual quote, (b) LOCAL q4 specifically reads 'defends churches from zoning attacks' not generic free-speech, so the vote does not map. (4) Bryant — HARD TRUE econ_q2: her own Facebook post May 15 2025 (https://www.facebook.com/kittybryantforcouncil/posts/122197453532266765) — 'This week, council voted to lower the city's real estate tax rate to $1.24 per $100 of assessed value. This is a change from our previous rate of $1.30… Council unanimously supported this decision.' Sparse-data caveat: ALL OTHER 29 cells NULL — she has stayed publicly silent on culture-war markers. NOTE: Trinity Episcopal Portsmouth (her employer) is a TEC LGBT-affirming mainline parish; vocational title does NOT clear Captain-voice Christian-conservative profession bar (per skill). (5) Hugel — HARD TRUE econ_q2: same unanimous May 2025 rate cut $1.30→$1.24 (he co-credits on https://www.markhugel.com). HARD TRUE pj_q0 (HIGH BAR cleared on MULTIPLE vectors): (a) Fraternal Order of Police Gosport Lodge #20 endorsement 2022 — direct FOP endorsement is hard evidence per skill, (b) 'participated in several Portsmouth Police Department's R.E.S.E.T. walks, visiting the neighborhoods where homicides have occurred' (city/campaign bio), (c) campaign plank 'Maintain competitive pay to provide for recruiting and retaining Police, Fire, and EMT personnel,' (d) IAFF Local 539 fire endorsement. Multi-vector affirmative law-enforcement advocacy. HARD FALSE pj_q3: WAVY candidate-profile quote that he will 'work with the police department, sheriff's department, social services department, and mental health department to confront crime in a holistic, efficient manner' + campaign-site 'prevention programs that keep young people on the right path' = explicit root-causes/holistic framing. Net 2 TRUE / 1 FALSE on cited cells. NOTE on 2A: Portsmouth's 4-3 2A Constitutional City resolution passed Jan 14 2020 PREDATES Hugel's Jan 2023 swearing-in, no vote on his record.

RICK-WEST 4TH-CONSECUTIVE-DEFER FLAG (URGENT): rick-west (VA Mayor of Chesapeake, R, level=local, top-priority in queue) remains slug-collided with rick-west (OK State Representative, R, archetype_party_default, level=state) — engine `by_slug` lookup is last-wins by bare slug. This is now the FOURTH consecutive batch to defer him (1047, 1441, 1625 on 2026-06-02 + this 1550 on 2026-06-04). Research is COMPLETE and stashed in `_dropped_rick_west_research` key of the 2026-06-02 0950 dossier — would have scored 67/100 (HARD FALSE bib_marriage q4 + christian_liberty q4 May 2024 first-ever Chesapeake Pride proclamation, FALSE refuse_overreach q2 COVID mask/distance/vax encouragement, TRUE econ_q2 unanimous 9-0 FY24 4¢ RE-tax cut, TRUE pj_q0 affirmative LE-pay-raise + Jefferies nomination, TRUE border q2 2017 8-1 anti-sanctuary vote, TRUE self_defense q1 Dec 2019 unanimous 2A Constitutional City presidency). REQUEST: please write a slug-rename helper (proposed: rename VA `rick-west` to `rick-west-chesapeake` since OK Rick West at archetype_party_default is the less-differentiated record; preserve OK historical references) — blocking a top-priority, fully-researched VA Mayor scorecard.

profile.religion: Catholic — congregant of Sacred Heart Catholic Church, Norfolk (per her campaign biography at https://courtneydoyle.com/about/).
profile.education: B.A. Government and Politics, University of Maryland (1987); M.B.A., Old Dominion University (1997).
profile.background: Vice President of Marketing, Sentara Health (27+ years). Norfolk School Board, District 2, 2013-2018 (chair 2017-2018). Norfolk Recreation, Parks and Open Space Commission, 2009-2013. West Ghent Civic League leadership 2001-2007. First elected to Norfolk City Council, Ward 2, May 1, 2018; re-elected unopposed Nov 8, 2022; current term ends Dec 31, 2026; running again in Nov 3, 2026 general. Married 31+ years to husband John, two step-sons; longtime Ghent resident. Officially nonpartisan ballot — real-world Democratic alignment (Progressive Voters Guide endorsed her 2022; Democratic-aligned VPAP committee profile).

courtney-doyle.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: Norfolk Council Ward 2, sworn July 1, 2018; re-elected unopposed Nov 2022; term ends 12/31/2026 and she is running Nov 2026. RELIGION: Catholic at Sacred Heart Norfolk (per campaign about page). EDUCATION: UMD undergrad + ODU MBA. CONFIRMED DEM-aligned despite I-nonpartisan ballot — Progressive Voters Guide endorsed her 2022 with note 'support for pandemic safety' + climate-leadership credit; ballot remains officially nonpartisan. HARD FALSE biblical_marriage q4: Dec 12, 2023 Armed Forces Brewing Co. council debate — Doyle stated promotional videos and social-media tied to the brewery 'do not respect the LGBTQ community, women or our Norfolk values,' explicitly treating LGBTQ status as a constituent of 'Norfolk values' (the rubric's state-endorsement-of-LGBTQ-identity adverse direction). Norfolk runs an annual city Pride Month program with LGBTQ Community Liaison office; Doyle has not publicly dissented from any Pride proclamation in seven years of council service. HARD TRUE economic_stewardship q2: voted YES on May 14, 2024 FY25 budget that CUT the real estate tax rate 2¢ (from $1.25 to $1.23 per $100) + 51% personal-property tax relief on vehicles $1k-$20k + 100% relief on vehicles ≤$1k. FY26 budget (May 12, 2026) maintained the $1.23 rate, vote 7-1, she was on the YES majority. HARD FALSE public_justice q3: campaign Safe Neighborhoods page (https://courtneydoyle.com/safe-neighborhoods/) describes her approach to gun violence as 'using the public education system, recreation centers, libraries, and community groups to impress upon youth that guns are not the solution' — textbook root-causes / public-health framing (adverse on q3). HARD FALSE self_defense q1: same page advocates for 'constructive and meaningful gun legislation that prevents guns from getting into the hands of those who shouldn't possess a gun' — affirmative gun-control framing (adverse on q1's red-flag / AWB / registry direction). SOFT FALSE refuse_state_overreach q2: signature platform issue is climate / sea-level-rise leadership ('positioned our city as a leader in combating climate challenges,' campaign flooding page; Progressive Voters Guide credit on 'climate change safety measures' is endorsement-bearing evidence on the ESG-embrace direction). NOT scored christian_liberty q0: she voted YES on Armed Forces Brewing Co. 6-1 explicitly on content-neutral free-speech grounds (her direct quote: 'I took an oath to uphold the right to free speech despite the fact I disagree with the speech'), but LOCAL q0 specifically reads as Christian-business religious-liberty defense — Armed Forces Brewing is military-themed not religious, so the vote does not map and the cell stays null per strict cite-only discipline (the Dec 2023 cite is preserved in the cross-cutting biblical_marriage q4 note above as the LGBTQ-as-Norfolk-value evidence). NOT scored public_justice q0 (HIGH BAR not cleared): Sheriff Joe Baron is listed as a personal endorser on her endorsements page but a single sheriff endorsement ≠ FOP endorsement; campaign Safe Neighborhoods page lists 'increased pay for Norfolk's public-safety team members ... to resolve recruitment and retention challenges' but this is routine governance budget language not affirmative pro-cop advocacy (the FXBG high-bar rule). NOT scored border_immigration q1/q2: Norfolk has no 287(g); no individually-attributed Doyle vote on sanctuary status located; ACLU-VA has pressured the Sheriff but the council-level posture remains undefined → null per discipline. NOT scored sanctity_of_life / christian_liberty q2-q4 / election_integrity / refuse_overreach q0,q1,q3,q4 / family_child_sovereignty / biblical_marriage q0 q2 — no quote-bearing positions located. 1 TRUE / 4 FALSE on cited cells = 20% — coherent center-left urban Democratic council member with one fiscal-restraint TRUE on the FY25 rate cut. Recommend revisiting after FY27 budget vote, any 2026 forum positions, and a focused dig into Norfolk meeting minutes for individual sanctuary / library-content votes.

4.note: Dec 12 2023 Armed Forces Brewing Co. permit debate: Doyle stated the brewery's promotional videos and social media 'do not respect the LGBTQ community, women or our Norfolk values' — affirmative endorsement of LGBTQ as a constituent of 'Norfolk values,' the rubric's state-endorsement-of-LGBTQ-identity adverse direction. Norfolk also runs an annual city Pride Month program with an LGBTQ Community Liaison office (norfolk.gov/PrideMonth), and Doyle has not dissented from any Pride proclamation in seven years on council.

2.note: Voted YES on May 14 2024 FY25 Norfolk budget that CUT the real estate tax rate 2¢ (from $1.25 to $1.23 per $100) + 51% personal-property tax relief on vehicles $1k-$20k + 100% relief on vehicles ≤$1k. FY26 budget (May 12, 2026) held the rate at $1.23 with no increase, 7-1 — Doyle in the YES majority. Multi-cycle fiscal restraint on the rate cell.

3.note: Campaign Safe Neighborhoods page describes her approach to gun violence as 'using the public education system, recreation centers, libraries, and community groups to impress upon youth that guns are not the solution' — textbook root-causes / public-health framing of violent crime (rubric-adverse on q3).

2.note: Signature platform area is climate / sea-level-rise leadership — campaign Flooding page 'positioned our city as a leader in combating' climate challenges; Progressive Voters Guide endorsement credit specifically names her 'climate change safety measures' work. ESG-affirmative direction even absent a single named climate-emergency-resolution vote, parallel to the Newbille FALSE on the same cell.

1.note: Campaign Safe Neighborhoods page advocates 'constructive and meaningful gun legislation that prevents guns from getting into the hands of those who shouldn't possess a gun' — affirmative gun-control framing on the red-flag / AWB / registry direction. Norfolk has not passed a 2A sanctuary resolution; the 2020 petition fell ~179 signatures short.

profile.religion: Christian, lay / home-fellowship preacher — Progressive Voters Guide candidate entry describes him as a 'Navy veteran and home preacher' (https://progressivevotersguide.com/virginia/2024/general/jeremy-mcgee). No specific denomination or church identified in public sources; he does NOT foreground faith on his campaign site or official council bio.
profile.education: Studied Computer Engineering, University of South Carolina (degree-completion not explicitly stated in official Norfolk biography).
profile.background: U.S. Navy veteran. Born/raised small-town South Carolina; relocated to Norfolk in 2005. Serial entrepreneur — built a high-school computer business, founded and later sold a family-friendly dial-up ISP. President and CEO of Radiant Digital (Norfolk-based digital-media firm, ~50 employees nationwide). With wife Malia owns The Inn and Restaurant at Four Eleven York plus several Norfolk real-estate ventures. Prior civic service: President, Ghent Neighborhood League; Norfolk Architectural Review Board; Norfolk Planning Commission; VisitNorfolk board. Sworn in to Norfolk Council, Superward 6 (citywide-style at-large) on Jan 1, 2025 after defeating Jeremy Cyril Mason 69.5%-30% on Nov 5, 2024; current term ends Dec 31, 2028. Currently co-chairs the Mayor's Housing Commission, serves on HRPDC, Erosion Advisory Committee, Coastal Management and Review Board.

jeremy-mcgee.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: Norfolk Council Superward 6, sworn 1/1/2025, term 1/1/2025-12/31/2028 — NOT on Nov 2026 ballot. RELIGION: lay 'home preacher' Christian per PVG candidate entry (no denominational specifics in sources). REAL-WORLD ALIGNMENT: Independent on the ballot but coalition is clearly Democratic — endorsed by Gov. Ralph Northam, Sen. Aaron Rouse, Sen. Angelia Williams Graves, Del. Phil Hernandez, Mayor Kenneth Alexander (all D), former Del. Jay Jones, former Del. Paula Miller (D), and EAN-PACE (teacher union); Progressive Voters Guide named him the more progressive choice in his race (with reservations about his tax-cut stance). Notably no GOP or single-issue conservative endorsements. DEVIATION FLAG: significant Christian-identity and fiscal-restraint signals counter the D-coalition pattern — 'home preacher' designation + Navy veteran + explicit support for the recent property-tax-rate reduction + YIMBY/Realtors/Chamber business backing = honest counter-archetype softener. HARD TRUE economic_stewardship q2: campaign Housing page (https://www.mcgeefornorfolk.com/housing) states 'I supported the recent property tax rate reduction and believe we should continue to carefully consider the impact of property taxes on housing affordability' (the May 14 2024 FY25 2¢ rate cut PREDATES his Jan 1 2025 swearing-in, so he didn't cast that vote himself), AND as a sitting member he voted YES on the FY26 Norfolk budget (May 12 2026) that held the rate at $1.23 with no increase (per Norfolk meeting docket). Two-vector fiscal-restraint TRUE: stated pro-cut preference + actual hold-the-line vote. Progressive Voters Guide explicitly flagged 'concern over McGee's support for reducing taxes that could help fund needed services,' corroborating his fiscal-restraint posture from the LEFT. SPARSE-DATA CAVEAT: ALL OTHER 29 LOCAL cells NULL per strict cite-only discipline — he took office only ~17 months ago, keeps a deliberately narrow policy lane (housing, basic city services, business-friendliness, fiscal restraint), and has stayed publicly silent on culture-war / Christian-conservative rubric items. NOT scored public_justice q0 (HIGH BAR): his stated 'funding, more staffing, and the right work environments for our first responders' lumps police with fire/EMS, no FOP endorsement located (only IAFF Local 68 firefighters + Sheriff Joe Baron personal endorsement, not the same as FOP); routine 'fund first responders' does NOT clear affirmative-advocacy bar. NOT scored biblical_marriage q4: Norfolk has annual Pride proclamations but no individually-attributed McGee vote or dissent located — null per discipline. NOT scored sanctity_of_life / family_child_sovereignty / christian_liberty / election_integrity / refuse_overreach / border_immigration / self_defense — no quote-bearing positions located. 1 TRUE / 0 FALSE on cited cells (=100% on cited but the denominator is essentially 1, which is the textbook sparse-data trap — the 100% reflects research diligence on a narrowly-focused first-term councilmember, NOT broad rubric alignment). Recommend revisiting after his first full FY27 budget cycle and any 2027/28 culture-war votes that may surface.

2.note: Campaign Housing page: 'I supported the recent property tax rate reduction and believe we should continue to carefully consider the impact of property taxes on housing affordability' (the May 14 2024 FY25 2¢ cut predates his 1/1/2025 swearing-in, so stated preference not personal vote). As sitting member voted YES on May 12 2026 FY26 budget that HELD rate at $1.23 with no increase. Progressive Voters Guide criticized him from the LEFT for his fiscal-restraint stance, corroborating. Two-vector restraint signal — stated preference + hold-the-line vote.

profile.religion: Catholic — inferred (not self-declared) from his 2010-2017 service on the Board of Directors of St. Mary's Catholic Cemetery, Norfolk (per professional bio https://petedecker.com/martin-thomas-jr/).
profile.education: B.A. Public & Urban Affairs, Virginia Tech (2002); J.D., University of Richmond School of Law (2006).
profile.background: Partner since 2006 at Decker, Cardon, Thomas, Weintraub & Neskis PC (Decker Law) — personal-injury, criminal, traffic, civil litigation practice. Won the Aug 23 2016 Norfolk City Council Ward 1 special election; re-elected Nov 8 2022 (79.3% vs Padraig-Eoin Leigh Dalrymple's 19.7%); current term Jan 1 2023 - Dec 31 2026. Elected Vice Mayor by council July 2018; re-elected Vice Mayor by unanimous roll-call at Jan 2023 reorganization meeting (term coterminous with Ward 1 seat). Civic record includes the Virginia College Building Authority (appointed by Gov. Ralph Northam, D) and the St. Mary's Catholic Cemetery Board (2010-2017). Wife Maggie, two sons; Wards Corner area Norfolk resident. Officially nonpartisan ballot — real-world Democratic alignment (Northam appointment + endorsed Democrat Angelia Williams Graves for VA HD-90 + appeared at McGee endorsement event with Mayor Alexander).

martin-thomas-jr.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: Norfolk Council Ward 1 since Aug 2016 special election (re-elected 2022, term ends 12/31/2026); Vice Mayor since July 2018 (re-elected Jan 2023). RELIGION: Catholic by inference from St. Mary's Catholic Cemetery Board service 2010-2017 (not self-declared in public statements). EDUCATION: VT public-affairs + Richmond Law. REAL-WORLD ALIGNMENT: Democratic — appointed to VA College Building Authority by Gov. Northam (D), endorsed Democrat Angelia Williams Graves for VA HD-90, appeared in McGee endorsement coalition alongside Mayor Alexander (D) and Sens. Rouse + Williams Graves; no GOP affiliation. HARD TRUE economic_stewardship q2: voted YES on May 14 2024 FY25 Norfolk budget that CUT real estate tax rate 2¢ (from $1.25 to $1.23) + 51% personal-property tax relief on vehicles $1k-$20k + 100% relief on vehicles ≤$1k. HARD TRUE public_justice q0 (HIGH BAR cleared): law-firm professional bio (https://petedecker.com/martin-thomas-jr/) explicitly states Thomas 'has successfully championed increases in pay for public safety employees' — direct affirmative-advocacy self-description; the corresponding Norfolk FY25 budget Thomas voted for delivered a 10%+ raise for nearly 300 police officers (https://www.wavy.com/news/local-news/norfolk/norfolk-approves-budget-with-a-more-than-10-raise-for-nearly-300-police-officers-750m-school-construction-plan/). Direct quote on his policy stance (campaign-bio): Norfolk 'must have a world-class police force' and the city 'needs to pay public safety officers well and create a culture and environment where they can thrive.' Multi-vector pro-police: self-stated championship + corresponding budget vote + first-tier policy quote = clears HIGH BAR. NOT scored christian_liberty q4: agent flagged Dec 12 2023 Armed Forces Brewing Co. 6-1 yes-vote but (a) Thomas issued no individual quote, (b) LOCAL q4 specifically reads 'defends churches from zoning attacks' not generic free-speech defense, so the vote does not map to q4; left null per strict cite-only discipline. NOT scored public_justice q3: agent considered FALSE on PD Chief Boone's holistic-framing budget posture but no Thomas-specific quote located; left null per discipline. NOT scored sanctity_of_life / biblical_marriage / family_child_sovereignty / christian_liberty other cells / election_integrity / public_justice q1-q4 / refuse_overreach / border_immigration / self_defense — no quote-bearing positions or council votes located (Norfolk simply did not take up Pride flag-raising votes, sanctuary votes, 287(g) votes, 2A sanctuary votes, or guns-in-public-spaces votes in his tenure that surfaced — the 2020 GOP 2A-sanctuary petition fell ~179 signatures short and the 2020 guns-in-public ordinance was pulled before vote). 2 TRUE / 0 FALSE on cited cells = 100% — sparse-data trap acknowledged but both TRUEs are HARD (FY25 rate-cut vote + multi-vector affirmative pro-police record). This is a textbook 'Democrat-aligned but conservative-direction on fiscal + police' counter-archetype softener and the public_justice q0 high-bar clearance is the genuine cross-track catch. Recommend revisiting after Nov 2026 re-election filing confirmation and FY27 budget cycle.

2.note: Voted YES on May 14 2024 FY25 Norfolk budget that CUT the real estate tax rate 2¢ (from $1.25 to $1.23 per $100) + 51% personal-property tax relief on vehicles $1k-$20k + 100% relief on vehicles ≤$1k. Operating budget passed unanimously; only Smigiel voted no on the CIP.

0.note: HIGH-BAR cleared on multiple vectors: (1) law-firm bio direct self-description — Thomas 'has successfully championed increases in pay for public safety employees,' (2) policy quote — Norfolk 'must have a world-class police force' and the city 'needs to pay public safety officers well and create a culture and environment where they can thrive,' (3) corresponding action — Norfolk FY25 budget Thomas voted YES on delivered 10%+ raise for nearly 300 Norfolk PD officers. Self-stated affirmative LE championship + matching vote = clears the FXBG-discipline high bar against routine-budget-vote credit.

profile.religion: Episcopalian — Director of Children & Family Ministry at Trinity Episcopal Portsmouth (a TEC / Diocese of Southern Virginia parish, mainline / LGBT-affirming). Vocational fact; not a self-declared Captain-voice profession of faith on the rubric's positive direction.
profile.education: B.A. Journalism & Media Studies, Rutgers University (2003); M.S.Ed. with secondary-education licensure, Old Dominion University (2009).
profile.background: Olde Towne Portsmouth resident since approximately 2010. Former newspaper reporter; former Hampton City Schools teacher and department head; since 2021, Director of Children & Family Ministry at Trinity Episcopal Portsmouth. Civic record includes President, Olde Towne Portsmouth Civic League; Portsmouth History Commission; Girl Scout leader; DAR; former CASA. Husband Hunter; three children in Portsmouth Public Schools. Won 2024 at-large council race; sworn 1/1/2025, current term ends 12/31/2028 — NOT on the Nov 2026 ballot. Officially nonpartisan ballot; full name on official city page is Kathryn W. Bryant (commonly 'Kitty').

kathryn-bryant.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: Portsmouth City Council at-large, sworn 1/1/2025, term ends 12/31/2028 — NOT on Nov 2026 ballot. NAME: Kathryn W. Bryant ('Kitty'), per official portsmouthva.gov page. RELIGION: Episcopalian — Director of Children & Family Ministry at Trinity Episcopal Portsmouth (TEC / Diocese of Southern Virginia, LGBT-affirming mainline parish). Per skill discipline: vocational ministry title does NOT clear Captain-voice Christian-conservative affirmative-profession bar (parallel to how an LGBT-affirming-denomination youth minister can't be inferred to oppose Pride proclamations without a direct quote). EDUCATION: Rutgers Journalism + ODU Secondary Ed. HARD TRUE economic_stewardship q2: her own Facebook post May 15 2025 (https://www.facebook.com/kittybryantforcouncil/posts/122197453532266765/): 'This week, council voted to lower the city's real estate tax rate to $1.24 per $100 of assessed value. This is a change from our previous rate of $1.30… Council unanimously supported this decision.' Direct self-quoted affirmative vote to CUT the rate 6¢ (from $1.30 to $1.24). Reinforced by FY27 budget (adopted May 12 2026) maintaining rate at $1.24 with no increase (https://www.wtkr.com/news/in-the-community/portsmouth/portsmouth-proposes-360-7-million-budget-with-no-tax-rate-increase-but-school-funding-falls-short). SPARSE-DATA CAVEAT: ALL 29 OTHER LOCAL cells NULL per strict cite-only discipline. She has stayed publicly silent on culture-war markers — abortion, marriage, Pride proclamations, family / school-choice, religious liberty (other than vocational employment), election integrity, ICE / sanctuary, and 2A — no quoted position located either direction in 17 months of council service. NOT scored public_justice q0 (HIGH BAR): public-safety language is generic ('we think about safety all the time'; promotes PPD's Crime & Gun Violence Dashboard); no FOP endorsement, no police-partnership program, no Romans-13 framing — does NOT clear the affirmative-advocacy bar. NOT scored border_immigration q1/q2: Portsmouth appeared on the May 2025 DHS sanctuary-jurisdictions list (later withdrawn) + Sheriff Moore canceled a pending ICE 287(g) agreement, but no Bryant-specific statement or vote located. NOT scored biblical_marriage q0/q2/q4: TEC LGBT-affirming employer context only — no Bryant quote. 1 TRUE / 0 FALSE on cited cells (=100% on cited; standard sparse-data caveat — the 100% is research diligence on a first-term councilmember with a deliberately narrow policy footprint, not broad rubric alignment). Recommend revisiting after FY28 budget cycle and any 2027 Pride-month or sanctuary-policy council action.

2.note: Direct self-quote May 15 2025: 'This week, council voted to lower the city's real estate tax rate to $1.24 per $100 of assessed value. This is a change from our previous rate of $1.30… Council unanimously supported this decision.' Bryant cast affirmative vote to CUT the rate 6¢. FY27 budget (adopted May 12 2026) HELD the rate at $1.24 with no increase — multi-cycle fiscal-restraint signal.

profile.religion: NO PUBLIC EVIDENCE FOUND — neither his campaign site nor official Portsmouth biography nor WAVY candidate profile identifies a denomination or church.
profile.education: B.S. Systems Engineering, U.S. Naval Academy (1977); M.S. Naval Architecture & Marine Engineering and M.S. Mechanical Engineering, Massachusetts Institute of Technology (both 1992).
profile.background: Career U.S. Navy 1977-2009; Rear Admiral (retired). Nuclear-Propulsion-trained officer (Rickover program); Surface Warfare Officer; Engineering Duty Officer. 100th Commander of Norfolk Naval Shipyard; final billet Deputy Commander for Logistics, Maintenance, and Industrial Operations at NAVSEA (oversaw ~26,000 civil-service shipyard employees). Post-Navy: Senior Vice President at CACI International (Norfolk office). Wife Luana; two adult children who attended Portsmouth Public Schools; Portsmouth property owner since 1986. Won the Nov 8 2022 Portsmouth at-large council race; sworn 1/1/2023; current term ends 12/31/2026; campaign committee filed as 'Reelect Mark Hugel For Portsmouth City Council Committee' — running again in Nov 3 2026 general. Council liaison roles: Military Affairs Commission, School Board, Economic Development, Port Authority, Planning Commission, Parking Authority, Crime and Gun Violence Reduction Commission.

mark-hugel.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: Portsmouth City Council at-large, sworn 1/1/2023, term ends 12/31/2026, running for re-election Nov 2026 (committee: 'Reelect Mark Hugel For Portsmouth City Council Committee'). RELIGION: not publicly disclosed — null. EDUCATION: USNA undergrad + dual MIT MS engineering. BACKGROUND: Retired Rear Admiral / 100th Commander of Norfolk Naval Shipyard; post-Navy CACI International SVP.
